name: Boxing Shadowboxing Rounds
description: Classic boxing-style rounds: 3 minutes of continuous shadowboxing followed by 1 minute of rest. Mix in all the punches and defense you know. Repeat for the number of rounds you choose (3 rounds shown).

exercises.note: One 3-min round; flow punches, footwork, slips and rolls. Rest 1 min, then next round.

source: General boxing fundamentals; public coaching references (standard 3-min round / 1-min rest format). Described in own words.

name: Boxing Fundamentals Drill
description: Solo, no-equipment skill session walking through the core boxing tools one at a time. Go through the sequence 3 times, focusing on clean form over speed.
source: General boxing fundamentals; public coaching references. Described in own words.

name: Beginner Striking Combos
description: Solo shadow practice of the most common beginner punch combinations, returning to guard after each. Repeat the set 4 times. Numbers refer to combos thrown per side where it makes sense.

exercises.note: Single jabs to warm up the line
Jab-cross (1-2): pair with the jab above
Jab-cross-hook (1-2-3): finish the combo with a lead hook
Slip after the combo, then reset to guard

source: General boxing fundamentals (1-2 and 1-2-3 combinations); public coaching references. Described in own words.

name: Muay Thai Bag Workout
description: Heavy-bag striking session for Muay Thai / kickboxing fundamentals. Requires a heavy bag and wrapped, gloved hands (and shin awareness for kicks). 3-min rounds with 1-min rest; 4 rounds shown. Cycle the strikes across the round.

exercises.note: Each leg; work into the round
Each leg; shin contact, condition gradually
Each side; then rest 1 min before next round

source: General Muay Thai / kickboxing fundamentals; public coaching references (standard bag-round format). Described in own words.

name: Muay Thai Fundamentals Drill (No Equipment)
description: Solo, no-equipment Muay Thai / kickboxing skill session. Walk through the eight-limb basics slowly for clean form. Repeat the sequence 3 times.

exercises.note: Muay Thai guard is similar; hands high

source: General Muay Thai / kickboxing fundamentals; public coaching references. Described in own words.

name: BJJ Solo Movement Drills
description: No-partner Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu movement session done on a mat. Builds the core grappling movements through repetition. Move slowly and correctly; repeat the circuit 3 times.

exercises.note: Hold and feel the closed guard

source: General BJJ solo-drill fundamentals; public coaching references (Grapplearts and similar solo-drill guides). Described in own words.

name: Karate Basics Drill (Kihon)
description: Solo, no-equipment karate basics session covering stance, kick, blocks, and a strike. Move with control and focus on form. Repeat the sequence 3 times.
source: General karate fundamentals (kihon basics); public coaching references. Described in own words.

name: Kata / Forms Practice
description: Dedicated form practice for traditional striking arts. Run a beginner karate kata and a basic taekwondo stance-and-kick pattern slowly and repeatedly, focusing on balance and clean technique. Specific forms should come from a qualified instructor.

exercises.note: Run your beginner kata several times
Each leg, stepping through the pattern

source: General karate and taekwondo fundamentals (kata / poomsae form practice); public coaching references. Described in own words.

name: Home MA Conditioning Circuit
description: No-partner conditioning circuit blending striking cardio with grappler-focused core and stability work. Reuses general bodyweight moves alongside martial-arts drills. Complete 4 rounds, resting 1 minute between rounds.

exercises.note: 2-min shadowboxing to raise the heart rate
Reused from exercises.json; substitute imaginary rope if needed
Reused from exercises.json; core for grapplers
Reused from exercises.json; then rest 1 min before next round

source: General martial-arts conditioning; public coaching references. Bodyweight moves (jump-rope, plank, mountain-climbers) reused from the existing NOOM/WebMD exercise set. Described in own words.

name: Judo Breakfall Practice (Ukemi)
description: Mat-based breakfall (ukemi) practice plus a solo hip-throw entry drill. SAFETY-FIRST: requires a proper mat, build height gradually, and ideally learn under a qualified instructor. Repeat the sequence 3 times.

exercises.note: Solo entry mechanics only; no partner

source: General judo fundamentals (ukemi and hip-throw entry); public coaching references. Described in own words.
